---
title: "Architecture Overview"
format: html
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  echo = FALSE
)

if (requireNamespace("micromort", quietly = TRUE)) {
  library(micromort)
} else {
  pkgload::load_all(quiet = TRUE)
}

safe_tar_read <- function(name) {
  store <- Sys.getenv("TARGETS_STORE", unset = NA)
  if (is.na(store)) {
    store <- tryCatch(
      file.path(rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::is_r_package), "_targets"),
      error = function(e) "_targets"
    )
  }
  tryCatch(
    targets::tar_read_raw(name, store = store),
    error = function(e) {
      message("Target '", name, "' not found. Run tar_make() first.")
      NULL
    }
  )
}
```

This page shows how the micromort package is organized: the data pipeline,
function hierarchy, user entry points, and development workflow.
All diagrams are auto-generated from package metadata via the
[targets pipeline](https://docs.ropensci.org/targets/).

```{=html}
<script type="module">
import mermaid from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs';
mermaid.initialize({
  startOnLoad: true,
  securityLevel: 'loose',
  theme: 'dark',
  themeVariables: {
    background: '#1a1a1a',
    primaryColor: '#2d5f8a'
  }
});
</script>
```

## 1. Data Pipeline

The targets pipeline processes risk data through five stages.
Target counts update automatically when plan files change.

```{r pipeline, results='asis'}
diagram <- safe_tar_read("vig_arch_pipeline_diagram")
if (!is.null(diagram)) {
  cat('<div class="mermaid">\n', diagram, '\n</div>\n', sep = "")
} else {
  cat("*Pipeline diagram not available. Run `targets::tar_make()` first.*\n")
}
```

## 2. Function Hierarchy

All exported functions grouped by category.
Click any function to view its documentation.

```{r concept, results='asis'}
diagram <- safe_tar_read("vig_arch_concept_diagram")
if (!is.null(diagram)) {
  cat('<div class="mermaid">\n', diagram, '\n</div>\n', sep = "")
} else {
  cat("*Concept diagram not available. Run `targets::tar_make()` first.*\n")
}
```

## 3. User Journey

Which function should you start with? Follow the decision tree below.

```{r journey, results='asis'}
diagram <- safe_tar_read("vig_arch_user_journey_diagram")
if (!is.null(diagram)) {
  cat('<div class="mermaid">\n', diagram, '\n</div>\n', sep = "")
} else {
  cat("*User journey diagram not available. Run `targets::tar_make()` first.*\n")
}
```

## 4. Developer Workflow

The 9-step workflow for contributing to this package.
Steps 4--5 follow the RED-GREEN TDD cycle.

```{r developer, results='asis'}
diagram <- safe_tar_read("vig_arch_developer_diagram")
if (!is.null(diagram)) {
  cat('<div class="mermaid">\n', diagram, '\n</div>\n', sep = "")
} else {
  cat("*Developer diagram not available. Run `targets::tar_make()` first.*\n")
}
```

## 5. Targets DAG

Auto-generated dependency graph of the full targets pipeline.

```{r visnetwork}
vis <- safe_tar_read("vig_arch_tar_visnetwork")
if (!is.null(vis)) vis
```
